title: Community Call to Action: Support Needed for Homeless Initiative
bodyText:
GEORGE NEWS - George Municipality Community Development section is calling on all organisations to join the effort to address homelessness in George and surrounds.

"Despite numerous emails and calls, attendance at meetings has been sparse, particularly from key departments such as Social Services, Correctional Services, and Education, as well as various non-governmental organisations," reads a statement released by the municipality yesterday afternoon.The Forum for People Living on the Streets (Plots) held a meeting on 12 June at the corner of St John’s and Varing Street. The forum, coordinated by the George Municipality, aims to ensure that essential services are provided to the marginalised and vulnerable homeless community."Several organisations, including Kidstop, SAHARA, Stop Trafficking, PASA, CREMHOG, George Night Shelter, and the Department of Justice, have been instrumental in addressing the issues faced by the homeless population in George. However, a collective effort is needed to make a significant impact," reads the statement."We need to think holistically about reducing homelessness," said Jonathan Gelderbloem, Chairperson of the Forum. "The homeless community, like any other, consists of various groups including the disabled, elderly, minors, parolees, and individuals with mental health issues."


> The initiative aims to tackle social issues, empower the homeless through skills programmes, and ultimately reunify them with their families and communities. The goal is to help them achieve sustainable livelihoods and become productive members of society.


Estelle Fredericks, who coordinates the forum and the project, emphasised the importance of involving more organisations and departments. "To make a long-term difference in the lives of the homeless and their loved ones, we need more organisations that work with families, children, and reunification services."A second parent training session is scheduled for July at George Municipality. This session focus on a skills training programme for homeless individuals with children and will cater for 15 participants, facilitated by Kidstop. The municipality coordinates all programmes and projects to avoid duplication of efforts.The George Municipality urges organisations to make contact and participate in these crucial efforts to ensure that the homeless community receives the support they need.

For more information or to get involved, please contact Estelle Fredericks on 044 802 2000 or Jonathan Gelderbloem on 044 874 2624.

‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’
